content.cn_text: 您将听到一段关于海豚的生物学讲座，其中关于海豚的观点分歧在于它们的
content.en_text: You will hear part of a biology lecture about dolphins and have views divide over their

content.cn_text: 智力水平。首先，您有时间查看问题31至40。
content.en_text: level of intelligence. First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细聆听并回答问题
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions

content.cn_text: 某些物种，例如宽吻海豚，它们的大脑甚至比人类的还要大。然而，
content.en_text: And certain species, like bottlenose dolphins, have brains even bigger than humans. However,

content.cn_text: 一些当前的测试表明，尽管它们的大脑尺寸与人类相似，但它们不具备与人类相同的认知能力，
content.en_text: some current tests suggest that they do not possess the same cognitive abilities as humans,

content.cn_text: 尽管大脑尺寸相似。例如，海豚经常被困在刺网中，
content.en_text: despite having a similar brain size. For instance, dolphins are often trapped in gill nets,

content.cn_text: 这些刺网最初是用于捕捞金枪鱼的。这些缠绕事件导致了不必要的伤害
content.en_text: originally intended for tunifying. These entanglement cases have led to unnecessary injury

content.cn_text: 或海豚死亡，逐渐减少了它们的数量。显然，海豚没有意识到
content.en_text: or deaths of dolphins, gradually depleting their numbers. Clearly, dolphins don't realize

content.cn_text: 它们可以跳过这些垂直的障碍物游到安全的地方。
content.en_text: that they could just jump over these vertical barriers and swim to safety.

content.cn_text: 人们常错误地将海豚永恒的微笑视为智慧和快乐的标志。
content.en_text: People often mistakenly view dolphins' eternal smile as signs of intelligence and joy.

content.cn_text: 实际上，这根本不是面部表情，而是它们头部的一种固定形态。
content.en_text: Actually, this is not a facial expression at all, but a fixed design of their head.

content.cn_text: 与拥有40多块面部肌肉的人类不同，海豚没有这样的能力
content.en_text: Unlike human beings with greater than 40 facial muscles, dolphins have no such powers

content.cn_text: 来形成表情。另一方面，小脑动物有时会
content.en_text: with which to form an expression. On the other hand, small-brained animals are sometimes

content.cn_text: 它们的智力水平被低估。鸽子和老鼠是小脑动物的典型例子
content.en_text: underrated for their level of intelligence. Pigeons and rats are typical examples of small-brained animals

content.cn_text: 它们可以完成人类都难以完成的复杂任务。
content.en_text: which can perform tasks so complex that most humans would have trouble with them.

content.cn_text: 在过去的几十年里，科学家们进行了无数次研究
content.en_text: Over the last few decades, scientists have conducted countless studies

content.cn_text: 这些被忽视的动物的认知能力，结果相当令人惊讶。
content.en_text: on the cognitive abilities of these overlooked animals with quite surprising results.

content.cn_text: 鸽子在镜子中看到自己的倒影时，会意识到自己的身体。
content.en_text: Pigeons are aware of their bodies when they see their reflections in the mirror.

content.cn_text: 鸽子还在特定条件下接受训练，并逐渐形成本能反应。
content.en_text: Pigeons are also trained under specific conditions and gradually form instinctive reactions.

content.cn_text: 在2009年的一项实验中，一组12只鸽子每次啄击笔记本电脑上的一个按键，就会得到食物和水等奖励。
content.en_text: In an experiment in 2009, a group of 12 pigeons was given a reward like food and water

content.cn_text: 所有鸟类都成功完成了这个技巧。
content.en_text: every time they pecked a key on a laptop. All birds successfully performed the trick.

content.cn_text: 这些测试结果表明，大脑尺寸可能不是决定智力的唯一因素。
content.en_text: These test results indicate that brain size is probably not the sole factor in determining intelligence.

content.cn_text: 另一个论点将注意力转向智力与大脑组成部分之间的关联
content.en_text: Another argument steers the attention to the correlation between intelligence and the constituents of brains

content.cn_text: 而非其单纯的大小。研究发现，海豚的大脑主要由脂肪细胞组成
content.en_text: rather than their sheer sizes. It's been found that dolphin brains are comprised chiefly of fatty cells

content.cn_text: 这些脂肪细胞在全身血液和中枢神经系统的细胞外环境之间起到保护屏障的作用
content.en_text: which serve as a protective barrier between the systemic blood and the extra cellular environment

content.cn_text: 它们也是神经细胞膜的重要组成部分
content.en_text: of the central nervous system. They are also essential components of nerve cell membranes

content.cn_text: 影响解决问题的能力表现。
content.en_text: affecting problem-solving skills performance.

content.cn_text: 现在，第二个主流观点是海豚具有高度智慧。
content.en_text: Now, the second mainstream view is that dolphins are highly intelligent.

content.cn_text: 对野生和圈养海豚行为的研究
content.en_text: Research into the behavior of dolphins in the wild and in captivity

content.cn_text: 为这些海洋哺乳动物的智力提供了令人难以置信的数据。
content.en_text: has yielded incredible data on the intelligence of these marine mammals.

content.cn_text: 研究表明，海豚不仅能作为个体学习
content.en_text: Studies show that dolphins not only have the ability to learn as individuals

content.cn_text: 而且这些个体还能将新知识传授给其他海豚。
content.en_text: but those individuals can then pass their new knowledge on to others.

content.cn_text: 我们来看一些典型例子。其中包括对比利（Billy）的测试，
content.en_text: Let's take a look at some typical examples. Among these are tested with Billy,

content.cn_text: 一只被困在海闸中并被救出的海豚。比利在康复中心度过了三周
content.en_text: a dolphin trapped in a sea lock and rescued. Billy spent three weeks in rehabilitation

content.cn_text: 并被放归野外。在那里，研究人员注意到比利开始用尾巴“走路”了
content.en_text: and was released back into the wild. There, researchers noticed that Billy had started tailwalking

content.cn_text: 这是一种只有模仿饲养员的圈养海豚才能掌握的技能。
content.en_text: a skill only mastered by captive dolphins who imitated the keepers. walking

content.cn_text: 比利并未被训练尾巴走路，而是通过观察康复中心的其他海豚学会了这项技能。
content.en_text: Billy had not been trained to tailwalk but had learned the skills simply by observing other dolphins in the rehab centre.

content.cn_text: 另一项研究涉及一只名叫凯伦的雌性海豚。
content.en_text: Another study involves a female dolphin named Karen.

content.cn_text: 凯伦接受了一项测试，每当她带来一块垃圾，就会得到一条鱼作为奖励
content.en_text: Karen was given a test in which she was rewarded with a fish for every piece of litter

content.cn_text: 她带给研究人员以最大化奖励。她很快学会了拿一份报纸
content.en_text: she brought to researchers to maximise its bounty. She quickly learnt to take a newspaper

content.cn_text: 把它放在水箱底部，撕成小块以获得更多的鱼。
content.en_text: keep it at the bottom of a tank and tear off smaller pieces to get more fish.

content.cn_text: 在过去的一百年里，研究人员已经证明海豚是极度社会化的物种
content.en_text: Over the past hundred years, researchers have proven that dolphins are extremely social species

content.cn_text: 并且已经进化出高度发达的大脑。
content.en_text: and have evolved to have highly developed brains.

content.cn_text: 这些因素是它们智力的最重要贡献者，并成为海豚的生存手段。
content.en_text: These factors are the most significant contributors to their intelligence and become the means of survival for dolphins.

content.cn_text: 2008年，研究人员追踪了一个约400只海豚的群体。
content.en_text: In 2008, researchers tracked a group of around 400 dolphins.

content.cn_text: 群体中有一只雌性海豚游泳非常吃力，总是翻来覆去
content.en_text: One female dolphin in the group was having a lot of trouble swimming and kept flipping upside down

content.cn_text: 或沉入水中。其他海豚围拢过来。
content.en_text: or sinking into the water. The other dolphins crowded around it.

content.cn_text: 它们背着受伤的雌性海豚，并排划水。
content.en_text: They paddled side by side with the injured female on their backs.

content.cn_text: 通过让受伤的雌性海豚保持在水面上，它们可能帮助它呼吸，避免溺水。
content.en_text: By keeping the wounded female above water, they may have helped it to breathe avoiding drowning.

content.cn_text: 那么它们是如何相互交流的呢？
content.en_text: So how do they communicate with each other?

content.cn_text: 科学家们通过观察和细致的测试发现，每只海豚都有不同的声音
content.en_text: Well, scientists have discovered through observation and meticulous testing that every dolphin has a different sound

content.cn_text: 类似于其他海豚能识别为特定个体的哨声。
content.en_text: resembling the whistle that other dolphins recognise as a particular individual.

content.cn_text: 海豚可以发出各种各样的声音。其频率范围是人类能听到的十倍以上。
content.en_text: Dolphins can emit a wide variety of sounds. The frequency levels range ten times beyond what humans can hear.

content.cn_text: 当然，除了声音，海豚还有其他交流方式。
content.en_text: But of course, there are other means of communication for dolphins besides sound.

content.cn_text: 在繁殖季节，雄性海豚在打架后会用鳍状肢轻抚雌性海豚，以确认社会联系。
content.en_text: During mating season, male dolphins stroke females with flippers after a fight to affirm social bonds.

content.cn_text: 第四部分到此结束。您现在有一分钟时间检查第四部分的答案。
content.en_text: That is the end of part four. You now have one minute to check your answers to part four.

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31-40.

items.title: Dolphins cannot find a way of escaping barriers such as fishing 31. _________ for catching tuna.
People wrongly thought dolphins to be smart and happy as they often have a 32. _________.
Small-brained animals such as pigeons and 33. _________ can perform various complex tasks.
Pigeons are trained to conditional responses and 35. _________ reactions.
Billy can imitate the keepers’ 36. _________ through other dolphins.
Karen hides a 37. _________ for more food.
For the past century, dolphins have been seen as 38. _________ animals.
Each dolphin produces a unique sound, like the 39. _________.
They touch each other to make up after a 40. _________.

groups.source_collect:

Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
Dolphin Intelligence
Point 1: Not highly intelligent
Research on the size of dolphin brains
• Dolphins cannot find a way of escaping barriers such as fishing $31$ for catching tuna.
• People wrongly thought dolphins to be smart and happy as they often have a $32$.
• Small-brained animals such as pigeons and $33$ can perform various complex tasks.
• Pigeons are $34$ of themselves.
• Pigeons are trained to conditional responses and $35$ reactions.
Counter argument
• Fatty cells in dolphin brains are linked to protective function and problem-solving skills.
Point 2: highly intelligent
Research on dolphin behaviour
• Billy can imitate the keepers’ $36$ through other dolphins.
• Karen hides a $37$ for more food.
• For the past century, dolphins have been seen as $38$ animals.
• Each dolphin produces a unique sound, like the $39$.
• They touch each other to make up after a $40$.
